codeReview.summary: The worker successfully cleared all 70 golangci-lint errcheck and unused blockers across 15 files in the deploy-readiness milestone. The implementation is a pure lint hygiene pass with no behavioral changes - exactly as specified by the feature description. All error returns are now explicitly handled using the `_ =` pattern, and 2 genuinely unused helper functions were removed (getAndValidateChallenge, pemEncodeBlock).

summary: PASS: The deploy-go-lint-hygiene feature is complete and correct. The worker fixed all 70 golangci-lint issues across cmd/auth, internal/auth, internal/proxy, internal/sandbox, and internal/server packages. Changes include: (1) errcheck fixes - adding explicit `_ =` handling for Close, Setenv, Unsetenv, SetReadDeadline, json.Encode/Decode, Signal, Write, and httpServer.Shutdown calls; (2) removal of 2 unused functions (getAndValidateChallenge from handlers.go, pemEncodeBlock from handlers_test.go). All validators pass: golangci-lint run ./... reports 0 issues, go test ./... passes all 4 packages, go vet ./... is clean, and go build ./cmd/... compiles successfully. The skill procedure was followed correctly with proper verification commands run before handoff. No shared state gaps identified - this was a straightforward lint cleanup that required no new conventions or documentation updates.
